div.p: User Manual

div.div: Getting Started
For HR Officers
For Employees
For Managers
Reference

div.p: User Manual & Guide
Module Version 17.0.1.0.0 | Macrofix

The HR Performance Review module provides a complete performance management system within Odoo. It enables your organization to conduct structured employee evaluations through review cycles, customizable templates, goal tracking, and multi-stage approvals.

div.strong: What this module helps you do:

ul.li: Create review cycles (Quarterly, Semi-Annual, or Annual)
Define evaluation criteria with weighted scoring
Allow employees to do self-assessments
Enable managers to evaluate their team members
Track individual performance goals
Maintain a performance history for every employee

div.p: Here is the typical flow of a performance review:

div.div: Draft
Self-Review
Manager Review
HR Review
Done

div.p: Each stage involves a different person. The review moves forward only when the current person completes their part.
The module has three roles. Each role can do different things:

tr.td: Every staff member in the company

ul.li: View their own reviews
Fill in self-assessment scores and comments
Manage their own goals
Everything an Employee can do, plus:
View team members' reviews
Score and comment on team members' performance
Start the review process for team members
Everything a Manager can do, plus:
Create and manage review cycles
Configure review templates
Generate reviews for employees in bulk
Give final approval on reviews
View all reviews across the company

div.div: Example: Sarah is a Sales Manager. She has the Manager role. She can fill in her own self-review AND evaluate the 5 people in her sales team. She cannot create review cycles or templates — that's done by HR.

div.p: When you open the Performance menu from the top bar, you will see these sections:
div.div: Tip: If you cannot see a menu item, it means your role does not have access to that section. Contact your HR department if you think you need additional access.

div.h2: 4 Setting Up Review Templates HR Only
div.p: Before you can start any review, you need a Review Template. A template defines what criteria employees will be evaluated on and how much weight each criterion carries.

ol.li: Navigate: Go to Performance → Configuration → Review Templates
Click "New" to create a new template

ul.li: Name — e.g., "Annual Review - Sales Team"
Description — Optional note about what this template is for
Departments — Leave empty for all departments, or pick specific ones
Job Positions — Leave empty for all positions, or pick specific ones

ol.li: Add Evaluation Criteria: Under the "Evaluation Criteria" tab, click "Add a line" for each criterion
Save the template

div.div: Important: The total weight of all criteria must add up to exactly 100%. The system will not allow you to save a template if the weights don't total 100%.

tr.td: Quality and timeliness of work delivery
Proficiency in required tools and technologies
How well the employee works with others
Clarity in verbal and written communication

div.div: Tip: You can create different templates for different departments. For example, the Sales team might have a "Client Relationship" criterion, while the Engineering team might have "Code Quality" instead.

div.p: A Review Cycle represents a time period during which performance reviews take place. For example, "Q1 2026 Review" or "Annual Review 2026".

ul.li: Name — e.g., "Annual Review 2026"
Type — Choose: Quarterly, Semi-Annual, Annual, or Custom
Template — Select which evaluation template to use
Start Date / End Date — The review period
Self-Review Deadline — Date by which employees must complete their self-assessment
Manager Review Deadline — Date by which managers must complete their evaluation
Departments — Leave empty for all departments, or select specific ones

ol.li: Save the cycle

div.p: Cycle Name: Annual Review 2026
Type: Annual
Template: Annual Review - General
Period: January 1, 2026 — December 31, 2026
Self-Review Deadline: January 15, 2027
Manager Review Deadline: January 31, 2027
Departments: (empty = all departments)

div.div: Deadlines matter! The system sends automatic reminder notifications to employees and managers 3 days before their respective deadlines. Set realistic deadlines to give everyone enough time.

div.p: Once your cycle is created, you need to generate individual review records for each employee. This can be done in bulk using the wizard.

ol.li: Open the review cycle you created
Click the "Generate Reviews" button at the top

A wizard will appear with options:
Departments — Filter by specific departments (optional)
Employees — Select specific employees (optional)
Skip Existing — If checked, employees who already have a review in this cycle will be skipped
Click "Generate"
The system will create one review per employee, each with evaluation criteria lines based on the template

div.div: Example: You have 20 employees in the Sales department. When you generate reviews for that department, the system creates 20 individual review records — one for each employee — each containing the 5 criteria from your template. That's 100 scoring lines created automatically!
div.p: After generating reviews, click "Start Cycle" on the cycle form. This will:

ul.li: Move all draft reviews to the Self-Review stage
Send email notifications to all employees asking them to complete their self-assessment
Change the cycle status from "Draft" to "In Progress"

div.div: Note: You cannot start a cycle that has no reviews. Make sure you generate reviews first.

div.p: After the manager submits their evaluation, the review moves to HR Review. This is the final check before the review is marked as complete.

ol.li: Use the "HR Review" filter to see reviews waiting for your approval

Open a review and check:
Are the self-assessment scores reasonable?
Are the manager scores and comments fair?
Do the goal achievements look correct?
Add any HR Comments in the "HR Comments" tab (visible only to HR)
Click "Approve & Complete" to finalize the review

ul.li: The final rating is calculated automatically (see Section 12)
The employee's profile is updated with their latest performance rating
An email notification is sent to the employee with their final rating
The review is locked and cannot be edited further

div.p: Once all reviews in a cycle are in "Done" status, go back to the cycle and click "Complete Cycle". This marks the entire cycle as finished.
div.div: Note: You cannot complete a cycle if any reviews are still pending. All reviews must be in "Done" or "Cancelled" status first.

div.p: When a review cycle starts, you will receive an email notification asking you to complete your self-assessment. Here's what you need to do:

ol.li: Open the review that is in "Self-Review" status

li.p: Go to the "Evaluation Criteria" tab. For each row, enter:

ul.li: Self Score — A number from 0 to 5
Self Comment — A brief explanation of why you chose that score
Self Summary — An overall summary of your performance this period
Key Strengths — What you did well
Areas for Improvement — Where you can grow

ol.li: Click "Submit Self-Review"

tr.td: Consistently exceeded expectations in this area
Often went above and beyond what was required
Partially met expectations; room for growth
Did not meet expectations in this area
This criterion does not apply to your role

div.p: Criterion: Team Collaboration (Weight: 20%)
Self Score: 4

Self Comment: "I actively participated in cross-team projects this quarter, including the warehouse optimization project. I helped onboard 2 new team members and shared documentation for our standard processes."

ul.li: Be honest — don't inflate or deflate your scores
Give specific examples in your comments (dates, projects, metrics)
Mention achievements that your manager may not know about
For "Areas for Improvement", suggest what you plan to do differently

div.p: Goals help you track specific objectives for the review period. Your goal progress contributes to your final performance rating.
Go to Performance → My → My Goals to see all your assigned goals.

tr.td: Deliver Phase 2 of the CRM migration
Lead at least 2 team meetings per month

ol.li: Open a goal from your goals list
Click "Start" to move it from Draft to In Progress
Update the Progress (%) field as you make progress (0-100%)
Add notes in the "Employee Notes" field to document your progress
When finished, your manager will mark it as Achieved, Partially Achieved, or Not Achieved

div.p: Goal: Close 15 sales deals this quarter
Type: Performance | Priority: High
Deadline: March 31, 2026
Progress: 80% (12 out of 15 deals closed)
Employee Notes: "Closed 12 deals so far. 3 more in final negotiation stage. Expecting to close 2 of them by month end."
After an employee submits their self-review, you will receive an email notification. The review then moves to the Manager Review stage for your evaluation.

ol.li: Open the review that is in "Manager Review" status
Review the employee's self-scores first. Look at the "Evaluation Criteria" tab to see what the employee scored themselves.

li.p: For each criterion row, fill in:

ul.li: Manager Score — Your evaluation (0-5)
Manager Comment — Your feedback and justification

li.strong: Fill in the Manager Assessment tab:

ul.li: Manager Summary — Your overall evaluation
Key Strengths — What the employee excels at
Areas for Improvement — Where the employee should focus next

ol.li: Review the Goals tab — Check if the employee's goals are up to date
Click "Submit Manager Review"

div.p: Employee: Ahmed Khan (Sales Executive)
Criterion: Job Performance (Weight: 30%)
Employee's Self Score: 4
Manager Score: 3

Manager Comment: "Ahmed met most of his sales targets but missed the Q3 target by 15%. His client retention is excellent, but new client acquisition needs more focus. Recommend pairing him with a senior account manager for mentorship."

ul.li: Compare the employee's self-score with your own assessment — discuss any big differences
Be specific and factual in your comments (avoid vague phrases like "good job")
Balance positive feedback with constructive suggestions
Reference measurable outcomes where possible (e.g., "delivered 3 projects on time")
Consider the employee's goals and their progress when scoring

div.p: As a manager, you can view the performance history of each team member from their employee profile.

ol.li: Open any employee's profile (e.g., from the Employees app)

Look for the stat buttons at the top:
Reviews button — Shows the total number of reviews
Goals button — Shows the total number of goals
Click either button to see the full list

On the employee form, look for the Performance tab which shows:
Last Review Date
Last Review Rating
Average Rating (across all completed reviews)
A history table of all past reviews

div.div: Quick view in Team Reviews: The list view at Performance → Team → Team Reviews shows all your team members' reviews with their ratings at a glance. Use the "Group by" options to organize by department, status, or performance level.

div.p: Each criterion has a weight. The weighted average is calculated as:
The Final Rating combines three components:

p.strong: Final Rating = (Manager Rating x 70%) + (Self Rating x 20%) + (Goal Score x 10%)

div.p: Goal Score = (Average Goal Progress / 100) x 5.0
Manager Rating: 3.85 (from the table above)
Self Rating: 4.10
Average Goal Progress: 80% → Goal Score = (80/100) x 5.0 = 4.0
Performance Level: Exceeds Expectations
Here is the complete flow showing who does what at each stage:

tr.td: Evaluates the employee and enters manager scores
Review is finalized. Rating saved to employee profile.
Goal is defined with target and deadline
Employee updates progress percentage and notes
Manager confirms goal was fully achieved (set to 100%)
Manager confirms goal was partly achieved
Manager marks goal as not achieved

div.p: The system sends automatic emails at key points in the process:

tr.td: "Your performance review has been initiated. Please complete your self-assessment."
"[Employee Name] has completed their self-review. Please proceed with your evaluation."
"Your performance review has been completed. Your final rating is [X.XX] - [Level]."
System reminder to complete manager evaluation

div.div: Note: Deadline reminders are sent as internal Odoo notifications (visible in the bell icon), not as emails. Stage transition notifications are sent as actual emails to the employee's work email address.

div.h4: Q: I can't see the "Performance" menu. What should I do?
div.p: Your user account needs the Performance Employee role (at minimum). Ask your HR department or system administrator to assign the "HR Performance Review / Employee" group to your user.

div.h4: Q: I submitted my self-review by mistake. Can I undo it?
div.p: Once submitted, you cannot undo the self-review yourself. Contact your HR Officer — they can cancel the review and reset it to Draft, which allows the process to start over.

div.h4: Q: Why is my final rating different from my self-score?
div.p: The final rating is a weighted combination: 70% Manager Rating + 20% Self Rating + 10% Goal Score. The manager's evaluation carries the most weight. See Section 12 for the complete formula and examples.

div.h4: Q: Can I see my manager's comments about me?
div.p: Yes, once the review is marked as Done, you can open your review from Performance → My → My Reviews and see both the manager's scores and comments. However, HR Comments are only visible to HR Officers.

div.h4: Q: I'm a manager. Can I change my evaluation after submitting?
div.p: No. Once you click "Submit Manager Review", the review moves to HR for approval. If you need to make changes, contact your HR Officer who can cancel and reset the review.

div.h4: Q: What happens if I miss the self-review deadline?
div.p: You will receive a reminder notification 3 days before the deadline. If you miss it, the review remains in "Self-Review" status. Your manager and HR will be able to see that your review is still pending. Complete it as soon as possible.

div.h4: Q: Can I update my goal progress after my review is completed?
div.p: Goals linked to a completed review should not be modified, as the final rating has already been calculated. New goals can be created for the next review period.

div.h4: Q: How do I create goals for my team members?
div.p: As a manager, you can create goals for your direct reports. Go to Performance → Reviews → All Goals (or use the Goals stat button on the employee's profile), click "New", and select the employee. Set the goal type, description, deadline, and success metric.

div.h4: Q: Where can I see my performance history across multiple reviews?
div.p: Your manager or HR can see your full performance history on your employee profile under the Performance tab. This shows your last review date, last rating, average rating, and a table of all past reviews. You can also see all your reviews at Performance → My → My Reviews.
HR Performance Review Module v17.0.1.0.0 | Developed by Macrofix
For support, please contact your HR department or system administrator.
